document.title: IEC 60601-1 National Deviations and IEC 60601-1-6 CB Scheme Test Report for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
document.documentType: CB Scheme test report attachment and national deviations
document.revisionOrVersion: Report No. REP028549; IEC 60601-1-6 report No. REP028549_1; User manual A-TCF-5-130 Version 0.1

sourceSummary.oneLine: Chunk documents IEC 60601-1 national deviations and an IEC 60601-1-6 usability CB Scheme test report for H&abyz digital flat panel X-ray detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory safety, usability, national deviation, CB Scheme, and product documentation evidence for medical electrical equipment submissions.
keyPoints: Canadian, Japanese, and Korean national differences were addressed for IEC 60601-1 safety requirements.
The IEC 60601-1-6 report No. REP028549_1 was issued by Nemko Korea on 2024-02-20 under the CB Scheme.
Usability engineering process compliance was verified against IEC 62366-1:2015+A1:2020.
The user manual for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW is Version 0.1 dated 15/09/2023 and is in English/Korean.

entities.name: H&abyz Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: Applicant’s name: H&abyz Co., Ltd.; Manufacturer: Same as applicant; factory address listed in Yongin-si, Gyeonggi-do, Korea.

entities.name: Nemko Korea Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: Name of Testing Laboratory preparing the Report: Nemko Korea Co., Ltd.; CB Testing Laboratory: Nemko Korea Co., Ltd.

entities.name: CSA Group
entities.evidence: Canada National Differences attachment identifies Attachment Originator: CSA Group.

entities.name: TÜV Rheinland Japan Ltd.
entities.evidence: Japan National Differences attachment lists Attachment Originator: TÜV Rheinland Japan Ltd.

entities.name: KTR
entities.evidence: Republic of Korea National Differences attachment lists Attachment Originator: KTR.

entities.name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
entities.evidence: Test item description: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.

concepts.name: IEC 60601-1-6 usability engineering compliance
concepts.evidence: Inspection of the USABILITY ENGINEERING FILE verified that the MANUFACTURER demonstrated compliance with IEC 62366-1:2015+A1:2020.

concepts.name: Canadian national deviations for IEC 60601-1
concepts.evidence: Canada National Differences are according to CAN/CSA-C22.2 No. 60601-1:08, CAN/CSA-C22.2 No. 60601-1:14 including amendment 1 and Amendment 2:2022.

concepts.name: Japanese national differences for IEC 60601-1
concepts.evidence: Japan National Differences are according to National standard JIS T 0601-1:2023.

concepts.name: Korean national differences for IEC 60601-1
concepts.evidence: Republic of Korea National Differences are according to Notification of MFDS No. 2020-12, Annex 1.

concepts.name: Use specification and risk analysis in usability engineering file
concepts.evidence: Use specification, user interface safety characteristics, foreseeable hazards, hazard-related use scenarios, and ISO 14971:2019 risk analysis were documented and passed.

evidence.claim: The IEC 60601-1-6 CB Scheme test report was issued as report No. REP028549_1 on 2024-02-20.
evidence.evidenceText: Report Number: REP028549_1; Date of issue: 2024-02-20; Test procedure: CB Scheme.

evidence.claim: The tested device is a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector with model references A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Test item description: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector; Model/Type reference: A1717MCW; A1417MCW; F1417MCW.

evidence.claim: The detector ratings include 3 A at 16 V, 6 A at 15 V, or internally powered operation.
evidence.evidenceText: Ratings: Detector: 3 A, 16 V or 6 A, 15 V or internally powered.

evidence.claim: The power supply box and AC/DC adapter are rated for 100-240 V AC mains input.
evidence.evidenceText: Power supply box: 1.0 A, 100-240 V~, 50/60 Hz, Cl. I; AC/DC adapter: 1.3 A, 100-240 V~, 50-60 Hz, Cl. I.

evidence.claim: The IEC 60601-1-6 report states that the product fulfills IEC 60601-1-6:2010 + AMD1:2013 + AMD2:2020 and EN 60601-1-6:2010 + A1:2015 + A2:2021.
evidence.evidenceText: The product fulfils the requirements of IEC 60601-1-6:2010 + AMD1:2013 + AMD2:2020 and EN 60601-1-6:2010 + A1:2015 + A2:2021.

evidence.claim: The IEC 60601-1-6 evaluation was a process standard review with no testing performed.
evidence.evidenceText: Summary of testing: Process standard only, no testing.

evidence.claim: The usability engineering file demonstrated compliance with IEC 62366-1:2015+A1:2020.
evidence.evidenceText: Inspection of the USABILITY ENGINEERING FILE verified that the MANUFACTURER demonstrated compliance with IEC 62366-1:2015+A1:2020.

evidence.claim: The instructions for use include a summary of the use specification required by IEC 62366-1:2015 AMD1:2020 Clause 5.1.
evidence.evidenceText: The instructions for use shall contain a summary of the USE SPECIFICATION ... Included in the user manual.

evidence.claim: The usability process included risk-control priorities aligned with ISO 14971:2019 Clause 7.1.
evidence.evidenceText: To reduce use-related RISK, the MANUFACTURER shall use one or more options in priority listed as required by ISO 14971:2019, 7.1; verdict P.

evidence.claim: The usability engineering process prepared a use specification covering intended indication, patient population, body/tissue interaction, user profile, use environment, and operating principle.
evidence.evidenceText: Use specification shall include intended medical indication, intended patient population, intended part of body or tissue, intended user profile, intended use environment, operating principle; verdict P.

evidence.claim: The manufacturer performed usability-related risk analysis according to ISO 14971:2019.
evidence.evidenceText: Identify USER INTERFACE characteristics related to SAFETY as part of a RISK ANALYSIS performed according to ISO 14971:2019; verdict P.
evidence.pageTarget: Page 8 of 15 and Page 11 of 15, Report No. REP028549_1

evidence.claim: The manufacturer established a user interface evaluation plan with formative and summative evaluation documentation.
evidence.evidenceText: USER INTERFACE EVALUATION plan established; formative evaluation plan and summative evaluation plan documented.

evidence.claim: The Canadian IEC 60601-1 national differences apply CAN/CSA-C22.2 No. 60601-1:08, CAN/CSA-C22.2 No. 60601-1:14, and Amendment 2:2022.
evidence.evidenceText: Differences according to Canadian National standard: CAN/CSA-C22.2 No. 60601-1:08, CAN/CSA-C22.2 No. 60601-1:14 ... Amendment 2:2022.

evidence.claim: For Canada, the power supply box and AC/DC adapter ratings of 100-240 V AC passed supply mains requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: SUPPLY MAINS ... The power supply box and AC/DC adapter are rated as 100-240 V~; verdict P.

evidence.claim: For Canada, protective earth impedance and current-carrying capability requirements were evaluated using appended table 8.6.4 and passed.
evidence.evidenceText: Impedance between protective earth terminal/appliance inlet earth pin and protectively earthed parts shall not exceed 100 mΩ; see appended table 8.6.4; verdict P.

evidence.claim: For Canada, leakage current allowable values were evaluated according to the Canadian Electrical Code and passed.
evidence.evidenceText: Allowable values shall be in accordance with the Canadian Electrical Code, Part I; see appended table 8.7; verdict P.

evidence.claim: For Japan, national differences are based on JIS T 0601-1:2023.
evidence.evidenceText: JAPAN NATIONAL DIFFERENCES ... Differences according to: National standard JIS T 0601-1:2023.

evidence.claim: For Japan, the power supply cord set was not evaluated in this report.
evidence.evidenceText: Power supply cord set for Japan is not evaluated, refer to the summary of testing in Part 1 original test report No. REP028549.

evidence.claim: For Korea, national differences include accepted national supply voltages of 110 V, 220 V, and 380 V, and the power supply box and AC/DC adapter rated 100-240 V AC passed.
evidence.evidenceText: Voltage National supply voltages are 110, 220 V and 380 V. The power supply box and AC/DC adapter are rated as 100-240 V~; verdict P.

evidence.claim: For Korea, supply frequency requirements were satisfied because the power supply box and AC/DC adapter include 60 Hz ratings.
evidence.evidenceText: Only appliances having supply frequency of 60 Hz or a frequency range including 60 Hz are accepted. The power supply box is rated as 50/60 Hz and the AC/DC adapter is rated as 50-60 Hz; verdict P.

evidence.claim: Korean instruction and plug requirements were marked not applicable and deferred to national language and cord-set requirements for the sales country.
evidence.evidenceText: Instructions and marking related to safety shall be in a language acceptable in the country sold; equipment shall be provided with a power supply cord set meeting national regulations; verdict N/A.

evidence.claim: The user manual applies to A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW and is Version 0.1 dated 15/09/2023.
evidence.evidenceText: User manual for A1717MCW A1417MCW F1417MCW A-TCF-5-130 Version: 0.1 15/09/2023 Language: ENGLISH / KOREAN.

reviewFlags.reason: This chunk begins mid-attachment, so early U.S. national deviation context is present through NEC/NFPA references but the jurisdiction header is not visible in this chunk.
The chunk contains multiple document sections: IEC 60601-1 national deviations, IEC 60601-1-6 usability report, Canada national deviations for IEC 60601-1-6, and a user manual excerpt.

Several requirements are N/A because the product was reported as not an X-ray system for certain IEC 60601-1 national deviation clauses, even though the later test item description is Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
